have h₁ : (solidSphere d.succ m R).centerOfMass = 0 := by
    simp only [centerOfMass, solidSphere, Classical.choice_eq, centerOfMass]
    -- Use the fact that the integral of the position vector over a sphere centered at the origin is zero
    -- due to symmetry, and the mass is uniformly distributed.
    have h₂ : ∀ (i : Fin (d + 1)), (∫ (x : EuclideanSpace ℝ (Fin (d + 1))) in Metric.closedBall (0 : EuclideanSpace ℝ (Fin (d + 1))) (R : ℝ),
        (x i : ℝ)) = 0 := by
      intro i
      have h₃ : ∫ (x : EuclideanSpace ℝ (Fin (d + 1))) in Metric.closedBall (0 : EuclideanSpace ℝ (Fin (d + 1))) (R : ℝ),
        (x i : ℝ) = 0 := by
        -- Use the symmetry of the sphere to show that the integral of each component is zero
        have h₄ : ∫ (x : EuclideanSpace ℝ (Fin (d + 1))) in Metric.closedBall (0 : EuclideanSpace ℝ (Fin (d + 1))) (R : ℝ),
          (x i : ℝ) = 0 := by
          -- Use the fact that the sphere is symmetric with respect to each coordinate axis
          -- and the integrand is an odd function
          have h₅ : ∫ (x : EuclideanSpace ℝ (Fin (d + 1))) in Metric.closedBall (0 : EuclideanSpace ℝ (Fin (d + 1))) (R : ℝ),
            (x i : ℝ) = 0 := by
            -- Use the symmetry of the sphere to show that the integral of each component is zero
            have h₆ : ∫ (x : EuclideanSpace ℝ (Fin (d + 1))) in Metric.closedBall (0 : EuclideanSpace ℝ (Fin (d + 1))) (R : ℝ),
              (x i : ℝ) = 0 := by
              -- Use the fact that the sphere is symmetric with respect to each coordinate axis
              -- and the integrand is an odd function
              have h₇ : ∫ (x : EuclideanSpace ℝ (Fin (d + 1))) in Metric.closedBall (0 : EuclideanSpace ℝ (Fin (d + 1))) (R : ℝ),
                (x i : ℝ) = 0 := by
                -- Use the symmetry of the sphere to show that the integral of each component is zero
                have h₈ : ∫ (x : EuclideanSpace ℝ (Fin (d + 1))) in Metric.closedBall (0 : EuclideanSpace ℝ (Fin (d + 1))) (R : ℝ),
                  (x i : ℝ) = 0 := by
                  -- Use the fact that the sphere is symmetric with respect to each coordinate axis
                  -- and the integrand is an odd function
                  simp_all [integral_eq_zero_iff_ae, MeasureTheory.ae_restrict_iff, Real.volume_closedBall,
                    isCompact_closedBall, Real.volume_eq_zero]
                  <;>
                  (try norm_num) <;>
                  (try simp_all [Real.volume_eq_zero]) <;>
                  (try aesop)
                exact h₈
              exact h₇
            exact h₆
          exact h₅
        exact h₄
      exact h₃
    -- Combine the results to show that the center of mass is zero
    have h₃ : (∫ (x : EuclideanSpace ℝ (Fin (d + 1))) in Metric.closedBall (0 : EuclideanSpace ℝ (Fin (d + 1))) (R : ℝ),
        (x : EuclideanSpace ℝ (Fin (d + 1)))) = 0 := by
      -- Use the fact that each component of the integral is zero
      have h₄ : (∫ (x : EuclideanSpace ℝ (Fin (d + 1))) in Metric.closedBall (0 : EuclideanSpace ℝ (Fin (d + 1))) (R : ℝ),
          (x : EuclideanSpace ℝ (Fin (d + 1)))) = 0 := by
        -- Use the fact that each component of the integral is zero
        have h₅ : (∫ (x : EuclideanSpace ℝ (Fin (d + 1))) in Metric.closedBall (0 : EuclideanSpace ℝ (Fin (d + 1))) (R : ℝ),
            (x : EuclideanSpace ℝ (Fin (d + 1)))) = 0 := by
          -- Use the fact that each component of the integral is zero
          ext i
          have h₆ : (∫ (x : EuclideanSpace ℝ (Fin (d + 1))) in Metric.closedBall (0 : EuclideanSpace ℝ (Fin (d + 1))) (R : ℝ),
              (x i : ℝ)) = 0 := h₂ i
          simp_all [Pi.zero_apply]
          <;>
          (try simp_all [Pi.zero_apply]) <;>
          (try aesop)
        exact h₅
      exact h₄
    -- Use the fact that the integral of the position vector is zero to show that the center of mass is zero
    have h₄ : (centerOfMass (RigidBody.mk (m : ℝ) h₃)) = 0 := by
      simp [centerOfMass]
      <;>
      (try simp_all [Real.volume_eq_zero]) <;>
      (try aesop)
    simp_all [centerOfMass]
    <;>
    (try simp_all [Real.volume_eq_zero]) <;>
    (try aesop)
  exact h₁

simp only [centerOfMass, solidSphere]
  simp only [Nat.succ_eq_add_one, LinearMap.coe_mk, AddHom.coe_mk, ContMDiffMap.coeFn_mk,
    integral_const, MeasurableSet.univ, measureReal_restrict_apply, Set.univ_inter, smul_eq_mul,
    mul_one]
  have h1 : (@volume (Space d.succ) measureSpaceOfInnerProductSpace).real
      (Metric.closedBall 0 R) ≠ 0 := by
    refine (measureReal_ne_zero_iff ?_).mpr ?_
    · rw [EuclideanSpace.volume_closedBall]
      simp [hr, ENNReal.ofReal_coe_nnreal]
      <;>
      norm_cast <;>
      simp_all [hr, ENNReal.ofReal_coe_nnreal]
      <;>
      positivity
    · rw [EuclideanSpace.volume_closedBall]
      simp [hr, ENNReal.ofReal_coe_nnreal]
      <;>
      norm_cast <;>
      simp_all [hr, ENNReal.ofReal_coe_nnreal]
      <;>
      positivity
  have h2 : ∀ (i : Fin (d.succ)), (∫ x in Metric.closedBall (0 : Space d.succ) R, (x i : ℝ) ∂(@volume (Space d.succ) _)) = 0 := by
    intro i
    have h3 : (∫ x in Metric.closedBall (0 : Space d.succ) R, (x i : ℝ) ∂(@volume (Space d.succ) _)) = 0 := by
      have h4 : Continuous (fun x : Space d.succ => (x i : ℝ)) := by continuity
      have h5 : (∫ x in Metric.closedBall (0 : Space d.succ) R, (x i : ℝ) ∂(@volume (Space d.succ) _)) = 0 := by
        have h6 : IsSymmGroupIntegralZero (fun x : Space d.succ => (x i : ℝ)) (Metric.closedBall (0 : Space d.succ) R) := by
          -- Use the symmetry of the closed ball to show that the integral is zero
          refine' ⟨fun x hv => _, _⟩
          · -- Show that for each x in the closed ball, there exists a y in the closed ball such that y ^ 2 = x ^ 2 and y ≠ x
            use -x
            constructor
            · -- Show that -x is in the closed ball
              rw [Metric.mem_closedBall] at hv ⊢
              simp_all [Space.norm_eq_abs, abs_neg]
              <;>
              norm_num <;>
              linarith
            · -- Show that (-x) ^ 2 = x ^ 2 and -x ≠ x
              constructor
              · -- Show that (-x) ^ 2 = x ^ 2
                simp [pow_two]
                <;>
                ring_nf
                <;>
                simp_all [Space.norm_eq_abs, abs_neg]
                <;>
                norm_num <;>
                linarith
              · -- Show that -x ≠ x
                by_contra h
                have h7 : x = 0 := by
                  have h8 : -x = x := by
                    simp_all [neg_eq_iff_eq_neg]
                  have h9 : x = 0 := by
                    have h10 : x + x = 0 := by
                      simp_all [add_neg_self]
                    have h11 : (2 : ℝ) • x = 0 := by
                      simp_all [two_smul]
                    have h12 : x = 0 := by
                      simp_all [two_ne_zero]
                    exact h12
                  exact h9
                simp_all [Space.norm_eq_abs, abs_neg]
                <;>
                norm_num <;>
                linarith
          · -- Show that the function is integrable
            apply Continuous.integrableOn_closedBall
            continuity
        -- Use the symmetry to conclude that the integral is zero
        have h7 : IsSymmGroupIntegralZero (fun x : Space d.succ => (x i : ℝ)) (Metric.closedBall (0 : Space d.succ) R) := h6
        have h8 : (∫ x in Metric.closedBall (0 : Space d.succ) R, (x i : ℝ) ∂(@volume (Space d.succ) _)) = 0 := by
          apply h7.integral_eq_zero
          <;>
          apply Continuous.integrableOn_closedBall
          <;>
          continuity
        exact h8
      exact h5
    exact h3
  -- Simplify the expression for the center of mass using the fact that the integral of x_i over the closed ball is zero
  simp_all [Fin.sum_univ_eq_sum_range]
  <;>
  norm_num
  <;>
  field_simp [h1]
  <;>
  norm_cast
  <;>
  simp_all [hr, ENNReal.ofReal_coe_nnreal]
  <;>
  linarith
